evals.prompt: Use the PostHog CLI skill to search the docs for feature flags and summarize the most relevant setup links.
evals.expected_output: The response should call docs-search through the wrapper and summarize the returned documentation without exposing the token.

evals.prompt: Show me a few PostHog event definitions through the CLI so I can see what events exist in this project.
evals.expected_output: The response should use a read-oriented project endpoint such as event-definitions-list with a bounded result size.

evals.prompt: Try to inspect the PostHog org from this CLI token. If the token is too narrow, explain the constraint and fall back to project-level tools.
evals.expected_output: The response should recognize or explain the project-scoped 403 behavior and avoid repeatedly failing org-level calls.
